_comment: Multi-CLI review. Each reviewer runs a ONE-SHOT command in its own pane (WezTerm/tmux). The pane gives the CLI a real terminal (some CLIs, e.g. agy/Antigravity, render only to a TTY and emit nothing to a pipe). The command is the CLI's non-interactive 'print' mode given the shared 'instruction', which tells the agent to review the diff and WRITE its JSON findings to {OUT} using its own file-write tool (we read that file, not stdout). Placeholders: {INSTR} = the instruction with paths substituted; the instruction itself uses {DIFF} (diff path), {PROMPT} (criteria+schema path), {OUT} (findings output path). Reviewers run with permissions bypassed so the file write isn't blocked. 'reconciler' runs headless (stdin) to merge findings.
instruction: Review the unified diff in the file {DIFF}. Follow the review criteria and the STRICT JSON output schema in the file {PROMPT}. Do NOT post anything to GitHub and do NOT modify any source files. Write ONLY your findings, as a single JSON object matching that schema, to the file {OUT}, creating it (overwrite if it exists). That file is your only deliverable.
